qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XML injection via unescaped comment data in XMLSerializer.serializeToString
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the exact same vulnerable code/root cause as the advisory: XMLSerializer.serializeToString does not escape or validate the data property of Comment nodes before serialization, allowing the --> sequence to break out of comment boundaries and inject arbitrary XML nodes. The finding references the same code locations (lib/dom.js:1735-1736) and demonstrates the identical injection pattern described in the advisory PoC.

selected_finding.title: XML injection via unescaped comment data in XMLSerializer.serializeToString

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The xml-injection-comment-serialization finding exactly matches the advisory-described vulnerability: XMLSerializer.serializeToString in @xmldom/xmldom 0.8.12 does not escape the comment-closing sequence --> in Comment node data, allowing arbitrary XML node injection. The PoC successfully demonstrated end-to-end exploit success: (1) comment data containing --> breaks the comment boundary, (2) arbitrary XML elements including <script> tags are injected into serialized output, (3) re-parsing the injected output yields DOM nodes with attacker-controlled attributes, confirming the injected content becomes part of the document structure. This satisfies both the exploit_success_predicate (demonstrated XML injection with structural DOM modification) and bug_activation_predicate (the vulnerable serialization path was triggered with attacker-controlled input).
evidence: Finding identifies the same vulnerable code location as advisory: lib/dom.js:1735-1736 serializes Comment nodes as buf.push('<!--', node.data, '-->') without escaping
PoC successfully executed and demonstrated all four injection scenarios: comment injection with -->, re-parsed DOM containing injected nodes, PI injection with ?>, and malformed comment round-trip
Serialized output contains proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF_XML_INJECTION_SUCCESS in injected elements, confirming attacker-controlled content injection
Re-parsed DOM contains <injected> elements with proof attributes, demonstrating structural modification of XML documents
Vulnerability allows XSS when output reaches browsers and data integrity violations in XML processing pipelines
Advisory PoC and finding PoC use identical attack pattern: createComment('--><injected ...><!--')

poc_executions.observed_effect: The PoC successfully demonstrated XML injection through unescaped comment data in @xmldom/xmldom 0.8.12. All four test cases passed: (1) comment data containing --> breaks the comment boundary and injects arbitrary XML elements including <script> tags, (2) re-parsing the injected output yields DOM nodes with the proof attribute EVOHUNT_PROOF_XML_INJECTION_SUCCESS, (3) PI data containing ?> injects elements through processing instruction serialization, and (4) the parser accepts comments with -- producing malformed XML on round-trip. The vulnerability allows an attacker to inject arbitrary XML nodes into serialized documents, which when re-parsed become part of the DOM tree. This enables XSS when output reaches a browser and data integrity violations in XML processing pipelines.
